statement.focus_question: Is there a graph of chromatic number $\aleph_1$ with $\aleph_1$ vertices such that for all $\epsilon>0$ if $n$ is sufficiently large and $H$ is a subgraph on $n$ vertices then $H$ contains an independent set of size $>n^{1-\epsilon}$?
statement.normalized: Is there a graph of chromatic number $\aleph_1$ with $\aleph_1$ vertices such that for all $\epsilon>0$ if $n$ is sufficiently large and $H$ is a subgraph on $n$ vertices then $H$ contains an independent set of size $>n^{1-\epsilon}$? What about an independent set of size $\gg n$?
statement.original:
Is there a graph of chromatic number $\aleph_1$ with $\aleph_1$ vertices such that for all $\epsilon>0$ if $n$ is sufficiently large and $H$ is a subgraph on $n$ vertices then $H$ contains an independent set of size $>n^{1-\epsilon}$? 

What about an independent set of size $\gg n$?
